## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime — split into
two types with disjoint responsibilities — plus the
`ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits actors implement and use.
The split deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in three ways,
all decided during review of an initial single-`RuntimeZC`
cut:

- Two types instead of one.
  - `ActorManager<S>` — actor catalog. Holds instances and the probe-name prefix; assigns ids; nothing else.
  - `RuntimeZC<S>` — message-moving infrastructure. Owns the pool, threads, channels.
- Actors are registered as instances, not as factory closures.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan called for `add_actor<F: FnOnce() -> A>(factory)`. `MultiThreadRuntime` needs that pattern because its `Actor` trait isn't `Send`; `ActorZC` already requires `Send`, so direct instance registration is strictly more ergonomic and the factory bought nothing here.
- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan had `RuntimeZC::seed`. Seeds are an app-level concern (this benchmark wants ping-pong; another might want a burst pattern); the Runtime just delivers what the app passes to `run`. Manager doesn't store them either — it has no notion of "kickoff messages".

### File-by-file

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-1` → `0.5.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`: new module.
  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Send to closed channel silently drops the message (its `PooledMsg`'s buffer returns to the pool).
  - `pub struct ActorManager<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — actor catalog.
    - `new(probe_name_prefix: &str) -> Self`.
    - `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` — instance registration, no factory.
    - `take_actors() -> Vec<Box<dyn ActorZC<S> + Send>>` — drained by Runtime at run time.
    - `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
  - `pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — transport.
    - `new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self`.
    - `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
    - `run(&mut self, mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented.
    - `run_no_probe(...)` — probe-clean; returns `Vec<u64>`.
    - `run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body taking an `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer; `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, so the orchestration stays generic without closure / `Fn`-bound complications.
  - Internal:
    - `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`.
    - `actor_loop_probe::<S>` / `actor_loop_no_probe::<S>` — per-thread loops.
    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.

### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).
- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).

### Design decisions recorded here

- Two-type split: `ActorManager` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC` (transport).
  - The runtime's job is "move messages efficiently"; nothing else.
  - The manager's job is "hold actor instances and assign ids"; nothing else.
  - The two only touch each other through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called. A future swap of either side (M:N runtime, name-based discovery) leaves the other untouched.
- Initial messages as a parameter to `run`, not state on either type.
  - `run` neither stores them nor knows what they're for — it just delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
  - The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message"; that knowledge stays in the binary rather than leaking into runtime types.
- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures.
  - `ActorZC: Send` is already on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send on the trait" benefit is moot.
  - `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
  - Future escape hatch (`add_with_factory(closure)`) is easy to add if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
- Generic `ActorZC<S>` / `ContextZC<S>` over the store backend.
  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
  - Type-erasure in `ContextZC::send` would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the measurement.
  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites unannotated.
- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
  - The runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later, predictably and once per dispatch.
- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
  - Function-pointer dispatch (`ActorLoopFn`) monomorphizes cleanly per `R`.
- Caller-supplied `Pool<S>`.
  - Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.

### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

## RuntimeZC: zerocopy ping-pong runtime (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime that the
`goalzc` binary and `goalzc-crit` bench will drive. Mirrors
`MultiThreadRuntime` (1:1 thread-per-actor, mpsc channels),
but messages are pool-backed `PooledMsg<S>` rather than the
unit `Message`. Two run entry points — `run` (tprobe-
instrumented) and `run_no_probe` (probe-clean) — apply the
goal2-crit lesson from the start.

### File-by-file

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-1` → `0.5.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`: new module.
  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — single method `handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`. Generic over the store backend with default `MutexLifo` so concrete actors can be written for one store or generic across stores.
  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>` and `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Sends to closed channels silently drop the buffer (returns to pool via `PooledMsg::Drop`).
  - `pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `{ factories, probe_name_prefix, pool: Pool<S>, pending_seeds }`.
    - `RuntimeZC::new(probe_name_prefix: &str, pool: Pool<S>)` — caller brings the pool so it can also inspect `free_len` / `size` from the outside.
    - `RuntimeZC::pool() -> &Pool<S>` — accessor for the same handle.
    - `RuntimeZC::add_actor<F, A>(factory) -> u32` — closure-factory pattern (matches `MultiThreadRuntime`).
    - `RuntimeZC::seed(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)` — runtime takes ownership; delivered after thread spawn, before warmup.
    - `RuntimeZC::run(warmup, measurement, pin_cores)` — tprobe-instrumented; returns `Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.
    - `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe(warmup, measurement, pin_cores)` — probe-clean; returns `Vec<u64>`.
  - Internal:
    - `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }` — channel payload.
    - `actor_loop_probe::<S>` / `actor_loop_no_probe::<S>` — the two per-thread loops.
    - `RuntimeZC::run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body; takes the actor-loop function pointer (`ActorLoopFn<S, R>`) so `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, which keeps the orchestration generic without `Fn`-bound complications.
    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.

### Tests added (4 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 26 total in lib)

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).

### Design decisions recorded here

- Generic `ActorZC<S>` / `ContextZC<S>` rather than type-erased buffer handles.
  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
  - Type-erasure (e.g. `Box<dyn AnyBuffer>` in `ContextZC::send`) would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the very measurement we built `BufRefStore` to enable.
  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites (`impl ActorZC for PingPong` works) while letting benchmarks parameterize.
- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
  - Matches the shape decided in the `0.5.0-0` plan and re-confirmed during pool review.
  - Runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — the buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later than the visible handler line, but predictably and once per dispatch.
- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
  - Function-pointer dispatch (`ActorLoopFn`) is monomorphized per `R`, so the actor loops monomorphize cleanly without runtime branching.
- Caller-supplied `Pool<S>` rather than runtime-built pool.
  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across iter_custom calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
  - Matches the pattern of "user knows the workload sizing".
- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
  - Matches `runtime.rs`'s `Context::send` shutdown semantics.
  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins". This is non-obvious because it depends on:
    - `Drop for PooledMsg` returning the buffer.
    - mpsc receivers dropping their queued messages on close.
    - main thread dropping its senders before joining.
  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.

## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime — split into
two types with disjoint responsibilities — plus the
`ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits actors implement and use.
The split deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in three ways,
all decided during review of an initial single-`RuntimeZC`
cut:

- Two types instead of one.
  - `ActorManager<S>` — actor catalog. Holds instances and the probe-name prefix; assigns ids; nothing else.
  - `RuntimeZC<S>` — message-moving infrastructure. Owns the pool, threads, channels.
- Actors are registered as instances, not as factory closures.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan called for `add_actor<F: FnOnce() -> A>(factory)`. `MultiThreadRuntime` needs that pattern because its `Actor` trait isn't `Send`; `ActorZC` already requires `Send`, so direct instance registration is strictly more ergonomic and the factory bought nothing here.
- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan had `RuntimeZC::seed`. Seeds are an app-level concern (this benchmark wants ping-pong; another might want a burst pattern); the Runtime just delivers what the app passes to `run`. Manager doesn't store them either — it has no notion of "kickoff messages".

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-1` → `0.5.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`: new module.
  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Send to closed channel silently drops the message (its `PooledMsg`'s buffer returns to the pool).
  - `pub struct ActorManager<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — actor catalog.
    - `new(probe_name_prefix: &str) -> Self`.

    - `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.

    - `new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self`.

    - `run(&mut self, mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented.
    - `run_no_probe(...)` — probe-clean; returns `Vec<u64>`.
    - `run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body taking an `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer; `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, so the orchestration stays generic without closure / `Fn`-bound complications.
  - Internal:
    - `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`.
    - `actor_loop_probe::<S>` / `actor_loop_no_probe::<S>` — per-thread loops.
    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.

### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).
- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).

### Design decisions recorded here

- Two-type split: `ActorManager` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC` (transport).
  - The runtime's job is "move messages efficiently"; nothing else.
  - The manager's job is "hold actor instances and assign ids"; nothing else.
  - The two only touch each other through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called. A future swap of either side (M:N runtime, name-based discovery) leaves the other untouched.
- Initial messages as a parameter to `run`, not state on either type.
  - `run` neither stores them nor knows what they're for — it just delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
  - The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message"; that knowledge stays in the binary rather than leaking into runtime types.
- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures.
  - `ActorZC: Send` is already on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send on the trait" benefit is moot.
  - `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
  - Future escape hatch (`add_with_factory(closure)`) is easy to add if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
- Generic `ActorZC<S>` / `ContextZC<S>` over the store backend.
  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
  - Type-erasure in `ContextZC::send` would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the measurement.
  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites unannotated.
- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
  - The runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later, predictably and once per dispatch.
- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
  - Function-pointer dispatch (`ActorLoopFn`) monomorphizes cleanly per `R`.
- Caller-supplied `Pool<S>`.
  - Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.

### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

lastPrompt: yes, it looks good and we'll see how it feels as we go, no doubt they'll be more changes.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

   The user is building `actor-x1`, a Rust workspace experimenting with the actor model (CSP / Hoare 1978). Their primary requests across this conversation:

   - **0.4.0**: Create `goal1-crit` and `goal2-crit` criterion benchmarks at `crates/actor-x1/benches/` mirroring the workloads of existing `goal1`/`goal2` binaries to cross-validate `tprobe`'s tick-histogram numbers against criterion's statistical sampling. Single-step `0.4.0` release.

   - **0.5.0 (multi-step ladder)**: Build `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` — a new multi-threaded zerocopy ping-pong workload where:
     - Messages are pool-backed byte buffers (not unit `Message` like goal1/goal2)
     - Actors get messages via `get_msg(size)` from a factory; messages return when dropped
     - Multi-threaded 1:1 actor-to-thread initially (M:N later)
     - Measure cost of sending messages between actors over time
     - `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` measure exactly the same work
     - Steps: `0.5.0-0` plan marker → `0.5.0-1` Pool + PooledMsg → `0.5.0-2` RuntimeZC → `0.5.0-3` goalzc binary → `0.5.0-4` goalzc-crit bench → `0.5.0` close

   - **Style preferences (durable)**:
     - Use intro + sub-bullet lists in chores/notes AND in doc comments (`//!`, `///`)
     - Apply `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, ...)]` on clap-derive fields with bullet doc comments
     - Always run install/retest as part of pre-commit (don't skip "if applicable")

   - **Architectural preferences for 0.5.0-2**:
     - Separate `ActorManager` (catalog) from `RuntimeZC` (transport)
     - Drop factory pattern — use direct `add(actor)` registration
     - "Seeds" / initial messages belong to the app, not the runtime or manager
     - Future: ActorManager itself becomes an Actor for control-plane work

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust 2024 edition, jj+git dual-repo workflow
   - `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` for commit/push/finalize lifecycle
   - tprobe: tick-counting performance probe with band-table reports
   - criterion: statistical-sampling benchmark harness with `iter_custom` for non-standard workloads
   - Niche optimization: `Option<Box<T>>` is one pointer at runtime (Box never null)
   - Zerocopy crate (Google's): `IntoBytes` / `FromBytes` traits (renamed from `AsBytes` in 0.8)
   - `BufRefStore` trait abstraction for swappable concurrency strategies
   - Rust trait dyn-compatibility: `dyn ContextZC<S>` requires concrete S
   - mpsc channels with `let _ = sender.send(...)` for closed-channel tolerance
   - Function pointers (`fn(...)->R`) are Copy + Send + Sync — useful for generic orchestration
   - Box<dyn Trait>'s default `'static` lifetime
   - `std::mem::take` for draining without borrow issues

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`** — version progression: 0.3.2 → 0.4.0 → 0.5.0-0 → 0.5.0-1 → 0.5.0-2. Added `criterion = "0.5"` dev-dep and `[[bench]]` entries for `goal1-crit`/`goal2-crit`.

   - **`crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`** — added `pub mod pool;` and `pub mod runtime_zc;` modules.

   - **`crates/actor-x1/src/pool.rs`** (created in 0.5.0-1, ~440 lines): 
     - `pub type BufRef = Option<Box<[u8]>>;` with niche-optimization documentation
     - `pub enum PoolError { SizeTooLarge { requested, max }, NoMsgs }` with Display + Error impls
     - `pub trait BufRefStore: Send + Sync` with `from_buffers`, `get`, `ret`, `len`, `size`, `is_empty` (default-impl)
     - `pub struct MutexLifo` — `Mutex<Vec<Box<[u8]>>>` + cached `size: usize`
     - `pub struct Pool<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` with manual Clone impl, `new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32)`, `msg_size() -> u32`, `size() -> usize`, `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`
     - `pub struct PooledMsg<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` with `{ buf: BufRef, len: usize, pool: Arc<PoolInner<S>> }`, Deref/DerefMut to `[u8]` (slicing `[..len]`), Drop returning buffer via `S::ret`
     - 15 unit tests

   - **`crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`** (created/refactored in 0.5.0-2, current shape):
     - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` with `handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`
     - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` with `get_msg` and `send`
     - `pub struct ActorManager<S>` — catalog only: `actors: Vec<Box<dyn ActorZC<S> + Send>>`, `probe_name_prefix: String`. Methods: `new(prefix)`, `add(actor)`, `take_actors()`, `probe_name_prefix()`
     - `pub struct RuntimeZC<S>` — transport only: `pool: Pool<S>`. Methods: `new(pool)`, `pool()`, `run(&mut mgr, initial_messages, warmup, measurement, pin_cores)`, `run_no_probe(...)`
     - Internal: `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`, `actor_loop_probe`/`actor_loop_no_probe`, `MultiCtxZC`, `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer type, shared `run_inner<R>` body
     - 6 unit tests:
       - `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down`
       - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown`
       - `run_no_probe_returns_counts`
       - `take_actors_drains_and_resets`
       - `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle`
       - `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix`

   - **`crates/actor-x1/src/runtime.rs`** — added `dispatch_batch(inner: u64) -> u64` to `SingleThreadRuntime` (probe-free, deadline-free) for goal1-crit's `b.iter` use.

   - **`crates/actor-x1/benches/goal1-crit.rs`** — sweep `inner ∈ {1, 100, 1000}`, `b.iter(|| rt.dispatch_batch(inner))`, Throughput::Elements(inner).

   - **`crates/actor-x1/benches/goal2-crit.rs`** — `iter_custom` per-sample fresh runtime, scaled to `measurement * iters / total_count`. NOTE: has known probe-contamination bug since it uses `rt.run` which internally has tprobe — parked, not fixed. The goalzc design applies the lesson by having `run_no_probe` from the start.

   - **`CLAUDE.md`** — added two sections:
     - "Writing style: prefer sub-bullets" — covers chores/notes AND module `//!`/item `///` doc comments. Includes clap-derive `verbatim_doc_comment` caveat.

   - **`notes/chores-01.md`** — sections added: "Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0)", "goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong — plan marker (0.5.0-0)", "Pool + PooledMsg (0.5.0-1)", "ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)" — all in intro + sub-bullet shape.

   - **`notes/todo.md`** — entries: criterion bullet moved to Done with [19] ref; goalzc/RuntimeZC In Progress with [20] ref.

   - **`crates/actor-x1/README.md`** — added Benches section pointing to `cargo bench --bench goal1-crit` / `goal2-crit`.

4. Errors and fixes:

   - **`#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment)]` issue**: User pointed out that another vc-x1 session had the same lesson — clap reflows bullet doc comments into prose. I had to look up `vc-x1/src/init.rs` (located via `find`) to learn the pattern and add it to actor-x1's CLAUDE.md.

   - **Doc-comment style mismatch**: User feedback during 0.5.0-1 review — "Please use the intro + bullet lists in code comments like in chores are described in chores-xx and todo". I extended the CLAUDE.md style rule to cover doc comments and refactored 8 doc comment blocks in pool.rs.

   - **Skipped install/retest**: User feedback — "I expect that fmt/clippy/test/install would have been run before my reviews". I had skipped install/retest steps. Now running them as part of every step.

   - **Vec<u8> vs Box<[u8]>**: User: "feels odd to use Vec<u8> as we know their size and it can't be changed?" I switched PooledMsg.buf to `Option<Box<[u8]>>` with separate `len: usize`, dropped the `Vec`'s redundant `cap` field.

   - **Variant naming SizeTooLarge vs SIZE_TO_LARGE**: User wrote `SIZE_TO_LARGE` (typo + style); I clarified Rust idiom is CamelCase and used `SizeTooLarge`/`NoMsgs`.

   - **Stack assumption in BufRefStore**: User pushed back on "BufStack" naming — wanted `BufRefStore` or `BufRefQueue`, methods `get()`/`ret()` not `pop()`/`push()` (ordering implementation-defined).

   - **PooledMsg Debug requirement in test**: `assert_eq!(p.get_msg(8), Err(PoolError::NoMsgs))` failed compilation since PooledMsg doesn't impl Debug. Fixed by using `assert!(matches!(p.get_msg(8), Err(PoolError::NoMsgs)))` and `match` blocks.

   - **`as usize` cast**: clippy `unnecessary_cast` since `pool.size()` already returns `usize`. Fixed by removing cast.

   - **`len_without_is_empty`**: clippy required `is_empty()` method on the BufRefStore trait. Added with default impl `len() == 0`.

   - **TProbe::name not public**: Test trying `results[0].1.name()` failed since `name` is a private field. Replaced with simpler `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` test.

   - **Factory pattern dropped**: User feedback — "I don't like the notion of an ActorFactory". Replaced `add_actor<F: FnOnce() -> A>(factory)` with `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor)` direct registration.

   - **Conflated runtime + catalog**: User feedback — "I see ActorManager separate from the Runtime, the primary job of the Runtime is moving messages as efficiently as possible." Split `RuntimeZC` into `ActorManager` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC` (transport).

   - **Seeds as runtime concern**: User feedback — "as for 'seeds' that seems more related to the benchmark itself and nothing to do the the runtime". Moved seeds out of Manager/Runtime state; passed as `initial_messages` parameter to `RuntimeZC::run`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Designed pool with multiple iterations to satisfy ergonomics, type safety, and benchmark fidelity (static dispatch end-to-end)
   - Resolved Drop's `&mut self` constraint via `Option<Box<[u8]>>::take()` pattern (with niche-optimization documentation)
   - Architecture cleanly separated: Manager handles registration/lifecycle, Runtime handles transport, App composes them with initial messages
   - Pool drain invariant codified in test: `pool.free_len() == pool.size()` after run (depends on PooledMsg::Drop, mpsc receiver drop, main-thread sender drop)

6. All user messages:
   - "acquaint"
   - "I want to compare our goal1 and goal2 benchmarks, which use tprobe, and compare those to criterion. So let's create goal1-crit and goal2-crit that measure the same work that goal1 and goal2 do."
   - "I think we should create the criterion benchmarks for the goal1 and goal2 workloads in `~/data/prgs/rust/actor-x1/crates/actor-x1/benches` so when cwd is `~/data/prgs/rust/actor-x1/crates/actor-x1` so `cargo bench` works, does that make sense?"
   - "SingleThreadRuntime::dispatch_batch(inner) is fine"
   - "Tool loaded."
   - "commit"
   - "y" (multiple)
   - "Looking at goal2-crit, is measuring my measuring by calling rt.run so they are not measuring the same thing"
   - "I have a new goal, goalzc, which has an ActorZC trait with a handle that takes messages of that are defined using googles zerocopy crate so something like: pub trait ActorZC { fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn Context, msg: zerocopy::AsBytes); }"
   - "1. Definitely whatever is latest, but it should probably just be `msg: &[u8]` 2. byte slice"
   - "1. Ideally actors get_msg(size) from a factory and use channels to communicate and messages are returned to the factory when they are dropped 2. multi-threaded initially 1:1 later M:N 3. measure cost of sending messages between actors for a period of time, so not individual sends initially 4. implement goalzc and goalzc-crit the goal is they measure exactly the same 'work'"
   - "1. Initially all messages are some MAX_SIZE 2. All are MAX_SIZE for now but later there will be sub-pools in the factory that handles which support various sizes 3. ms 0.5.0 4. Yes create new RuntimeZC"
   - "How do I run the pool tests"
   - "I want get_msg to return a Result with SIZE_TO_LARGE or NO_MSGS errors and Pool::new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32) -> Self"
   - "agree with you suggestions!"
   - "I'm confused get_msg is returning: Ok(PooledMsg { buf: Some(buf), pool: self.inner.clone(), }) I'm expecting it to return something like: Ok(&mut [u8]) that can then be the 'memory' for some zerocopy message."
   - "The egronomics look good, and I now see pub struct PooledMsg and I think the 'self.inner.clone()' is incrementing the Arc and buf, I hope is a, &mut [u8]. Since these are fixed size arrays. But overall it feels odd that to use Vec<u8> as we know their size and it can't be changed?"
   - "Still talking no code changes; When I said 'atomic stack' I meant it from a logical pov, and I want to be able to change and benchmark so I can compare different implementations of 'atomic stack' or it could even be a ring_buffer or whatever. Also how about we create something like `Type BufRef = Option<Box<[u8]>>;` with a nice comment that this is a safe NULL, non-null pointer, thoughts"
   - "(A.) is fine but I in actual fact we shouldn't assume a stack maybe BufRefQueue or BufRefStore with two fns get()/ret(). These can be implemented however the trait wants to"
   - "I think trait BufRefStore we should have a `fn size() -> usize` that returns the max size and a test can validate that the BufRefStore matches the Pool size. Note in the future a Pool will have multi fixed sizes so if 'most' of the msgs are small there can be many relative to fewer large buffers. In this caase BufRefStore.size() is the total number of buffers in the Pool."
   - "Please use the intro + bullet lists in code comments like in chores are described in chores-xx and todo"
   - "but the instance of you working on vc-x1 just said that clap is changing those bullet lists into paragraphs they had to add #[arg(.., verbatium_doc-comment), ..] before the comment. See: ../../../vc-x1/CLAUDE.md and ../../../vc-x1/src/init.rs"
   - "First you hadn't installed 0.5.0-1, I expect that fmt/clippy/test/install would have been run bfore my reviews. Also can we do a quick test and see how the our doc and fn comments look? Also, we'll want to update goal1&2 (not now) but when creating goalzc of course use the new style."
   - "SG, ready to commit"
   - "contine"
   - "I don't like the notion of an ActorFactory that implies actors are similar, that happens to be the case for these trivial tests. I'd rather use a ActorManager model where you an user/bot creates an actor that executes some State Machine definition and adds gives an instance to the manager for other enties can then discover and connect with. I suspect the Manager will have a method based API but also be an Actor itself likely it'll be multiple actors for instance an actor for communicating to a GUI another for CLI another to drain logs or gather probe info ... So for now a simple method API for a simple ActorManager, thoughts."
   - "I see ActorManager separate from the Runtime, the primary job of the Runtime is moving messages as efficiently as possible. The ActorManager doesn't care about the Runtime other that what services it needs they need to interact with each other."
   - "Seems like a reasonable start, as for 'seeds' that seems more related to the benchmark itself and nothing to do the the runtime, neither the factory nor runtime knows what a benchmark is that i.e. it's the 'app'. The 'app' decides what factory and what runtime to use. Those area all conflated in goal1 and goal2 but in goalzc we're exploring a more realistic model, may not work but that's how we learn."
   - "yes, it looks good and we'll see how it feels as we go, no doubt they'll be more changes."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **0.5.0-2** (currently in progress): Final review and commit pending. Code/tests/notes done on disk.

   - **0.5.0-4**: `benches/goalzc-crit.rs` — Criterion `iter_custom` over `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`.
   - **0.5.0**: Closing — drop -N suffix, update README Binaries+Benches, move todo.md entries to Done.
   - Also: `goal2-crit` probe-contamination fix (parked, may be revisited later)
   - Also: refactor `goal1.rs`/`goal2.rs` to new bullet style (user said "not now" but eventually)

8. Current Work:
   I had just completed the 0.5.0-2 refactor for `runtime_zc.rs` per user's architectural feedback (split into ActorManager + RuntimeZC; drop factory; move seeds to app). Specifically:
   
   - Refactored `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs` to have two separate types: `ActorManager<S>` (catalog) and `RuntimeZC<S>` (transport). 
   - `ActorManager`: `new(prefix)`, `add(actor)`, `take_actors()`, `probe_name_prefix()`

   The next thing I would do is summarize for review (per the "review before proposing the commit block" rule in CLAUDE.md) and wait for user approval before proposing commit title/body.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Summarize the 0.5.0-2 work for user review and wait for approval before proposing commit message. The user's last message was "yes, it looks good and we'll see how it feels as we go, no doubt they'll be more changes." which approved the design refactor. After implementing it (the work is done on disk), per CLAUDE.md "After finishing a unit of work, summarize what changed and stop there. Do not pre-emptively lay out the Checkpoint-1 commit commands. Wait for the user to signal review is complete before proposing the commit block."

   The summary message to write would describe what was done in 0.5.0-2:
   - `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: 0.5.0-1 → 0.5.0-2

   - `notes/chores-01.md`: new section
   - 6 new runtime_zc tests, 28 total passing
   - fmt/clippy clean, install + retest done (binaries report 0.5.0-2)
   
   Then wait for user review approval before proposing the commit title `feat: ActorManager + RuntimeZC zerocopy runtime (0.5.0-2)` and body.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/wink/.claude/projects/-home-wink-data-prgs-rust-actor-x1/a6e6a96c-23b5-41ba-9f86-8b77b69bef2f.jsonl

[package]
name = "actor-x1"
version = "0.5.0-2"
edition = "2024"
description = "An experiment in the actor model in Rust (Communicating Sequential Processes, Hoare 1978)."
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"

[dependencies]
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "wrap_help"] }
tprobe = { path = "../tprobe" }

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = "0.5"

[[bench]]
name = "goal1-crit"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "goal2-crit"
harness = false

0.5.0-2 work is on disk. Summary of what changed:

**`crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`** — bumped 0.5.0-1 → 0.5.0-2.

**`crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`** — added `pub mod runtime_zc;`.

**`crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`** (new) — split cleanly into:

- `RuntimeZC<S>` — transport only: `new(pool)`, `pool()`, `run(&mut mgr, initial_messages, warmup, measurement, pin_cores)`, `run_no_probe(...)`.
- Direct `add(actor)` registration — factory pattern dropped.

- 6 tests: `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down`, `pool_is_full_after_shutdown`, `run_no_probe_returns_counts`, `take_actors_drains_and_resets`, `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle`, `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix`.

1962	## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)
1963	
1964	Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime — split into
1965	two types with disjoint responsibilities — plus the
1966	`ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits actors implement and use.
1967	The split deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in three ways,
1968	all decided during review of an initial single-`RuntimeZC`
1969	cut:
1970	
1971	- Two types instead of one.
1972	  - `ActorManager<S>` — actor catalog. Holds instances and the probe-name prefix; assigns ids; nothing else.
1973	  - `RuntimeZC<S>` — message-moving infrastructure. Owns the pool, threads, channels.
1974	- Actors are registered as instances, not as factory closures.
1975	  - The `0.5.0-0` plan called for `add_actor<F: FnOnce() -> A>(factory)`. `MultiThreadRuntime` needs that pattern because its `Actor` trait isn't `Send`; `ActorZC` already requires `Send`, so direct instance registration is strictly more ergonomic and the factory bought nothing here.
1976	- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime.
1977	  - The `0.5.0-0` plan had `RuntimeZC::seed`. Seeds are an app-level concern (this benchmark wants ping-pong; another might want a burst pattern); the Runtime just delivers what the app passes to `run`. Manager doesn't store them either — it has no notion of "kickoff messages".
1978	
1979	### File-by-file
1980	
1981	- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-1` → `0.5.0-2`.
1982	- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
1983	- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`: new module.
1984	  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
1985	  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Send to closed channel silently drops the message (its `PooledMsg`'s buffer returns to the pool).
1986	  - `pub struct ActorManager<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — actor catalog.
1987	    - `new(probe_name_prefix: &str) -> Self`.
1988	    - `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` — instance registration, no factory.
1989	    - `take_actors() -> Vec<Box<dyn ActorZC<S> + Send>>` — drained by Runtime at run time.
1990	    - `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
1991	  - `pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — transport.
1992	    - `new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self`.
1993	    - `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
1994	    - `run(&mut self, mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented.
1995	    - `run_no_probe(...)` — probe-clean; returns `Vec<u64>`.
1996	    - `run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body taking an `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer; `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, so the orchestration stays generic without closure / `Fn`-bound complications.
1997	  - Internal:
1998	    - `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`.
1999	    - `actor_loop_probe::<S>` / `actor_loop_no_probe::<S>` — per-thread loops.
2000	    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.
2001	
2002	### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)
2003	
2004	- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
2005	- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
2006	- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
2007	- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
2008	- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).
2009	- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).
2010	
2011	### Design decisions recorded here
2012	
2013	- Two-type split: `ActorManager` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC` (transport).
2014	  - The runtime's job is "move messages efficiently"; nothing else.
2015	  - The manager's job is "hold actor instances and assign ids"; nothing else.
2016	  - The two only touch each other through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called. A future swap of either side (M:N runtime, name-based discovery) leaves the other untouched.
2017	- Initial messages as a parameter to `run`, not state on either type.
2018	  - `run` neither stores them nor knows what they're for — it just delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
2019	  - The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message"; that knowledge stays in the binary rather than leaking into runtime types.
2020	- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures.
2021	  - `ActorZC: Send` is already on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send on the trait" benefit is moot.
2022	  - `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
2023	  - Future escape hatch (`add_with_factory(closure)`) is easy to add if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
2024	- Generic `ActorZC<S>` / `ContextZC<S>` over the store backend.
2025	  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
2026	  - Type-erasure in `ContextZC::send` would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the measurement.
2027	  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites unannotated.
2028	- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
2029	  - The runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later, predictably and once per dispatch.
2030	- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
2031	  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
2032	  - Function-pointer dispatch (`ActorLoopFn`) monomorphizes cleanly per `R`.
2033	- Caller-supplied `Pool<S>`.
2034	  - Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
2035	  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
2036	  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
2037	- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
2038	  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
2039	  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
2040	- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
2041	  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
2042	- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
2043	  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
2044	  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
2045	  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.
2046	
2047	### Future direction (noted, not built today)
2048	
2049	- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
2050	- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
2051	- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
2052	- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
2053	- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
2054	
2055	## Future work: linkme/inventory benchmark harness
2056	
2057	Idea captured during `0.3.0-0`; not scheduled for
2058	implementation yet. Explores using compile-time static
2059	registration (`linkme` distributed slices or `inventory` via
2060	`ctor`) to build a `tprobe`-driven benchmark runner where
2061	individual benchmarks declare themselves in separate files
2062	and the runner picks them up with no `main()` edits per
2063	benchmark — the ergonomic pattern criterion-rs offers.
2064	
2065	### Fit for benchmarks
2066	
2067	The bot thinks this is a strong fit. Each benchmark file
2068	drops in a `bench!(...)` invocation; the runner iterates
2069	the registered entries, sets up warmup/calibration/pin
2070	config once, and prints a `tprobe` band table per
2071	benchmark. The measurement primitive already exists
2072	(`tprobe`); the missing piece is the registration and
2073	iteration layer. Result: adding a benchmark is a
2074	one-file change with no central table to edit.
2075	
2076	### Fit for actor applications
2077	
2078	The bot thinks this is a partial fit. Registration gives
2079	discovery ("enumerate every `Actor` type the binary
2080	knows"), but actor construction still needs runtime config
2081	— id, name, channel endpoints, thread placement — which

file.content:
## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime — split into
two types with disjoint responsibilities — plus the
`ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits actors implement and use.
The split deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in three ways,
all decided during review of an initial single-`RuntimeZC`
cut:

- Two types instead of one.
  - `ActorManager<S>` — actor catalog. Holds instances and the probe-name prefix; assigns ids; nothing else.
  - `RuntimeZC<S>` — message-moving infrastructure. Owns the pool, threads, channels.
- Actors are registered as instances, not as factory closures.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan called for `add_actor<F: FnOnce() -> A>(factory)`. `MultiThreadRuntime` needs that pattern because its `Actor` trait isn't `Send`; `ActorZC` already requires `Send`, so direct instance registration is strictly more ergonomic and the factory bought nothing here.
- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime.
  - The `0.5.0-0` plan had `RuntimeZC::seed`. Seeds are an app-level concern (this benchmark wants ping-pong; another might want a burst pattern); the Runtime just delivers what the app passes to `run`. Manager doesn't store them either — it has no notion of "kickoff messages".

### File-by-file

- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`: `0.5.0-1` → `0.5.0-2`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`: new module.
  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Send to closed channel silently drops the message (its `PooledMsg`'s buffer returns to the pool).
  - `pub struct ActorManager<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — actor catalog.
    - `new(probe_name_prefix: &str) -> Self`.
    - `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` — instance registration, no factory.
    - `take_actors() -> Vec<Box<dyn ActorZC<S> + Send>>` — drained by Runtime at run time.
    - `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
  - `pub struct RuntimeZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — transport.
    - `new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self`.
    - `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
    - `run(&mut self, mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented.
    - `run_no_probe(...)` — probe-clean; returns `Vec<u64>`.
    - `run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body taking an `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer; `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, so the orchestration stays generic without closure / `Fn`-bound complications.
  - Internal:
    - `enum SignalZC<S> { User(PooledMsg<S>), ClearProbe, Shutdown }`.
    - `actor_loop_probe::<S>` / `actor_loop_no_probe::<S>` — per-thread loops.
    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.

### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).
- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).

### Design decisions recorded here

- Two-type split: `ActorManager` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC` (transport).
  - The runtime's job is "move messages efficiently"; nothing else.
  - The manager's job is "hold actor instances and assign ids"; nothing else.
  - The two only touch each other through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called. A future swap of either side (M:N runtime, name-based discovery) leaves the other untouched.
- Initial messages as a parameter to `run`, not state on either type.
  - `run` neither stores them nor knows what they're for — it just delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
  - The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message"; that knowledge stays in the binary rather than leaking into runtime types.
- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures.
  - `ActorZC: Send` is already on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send on the trait" benefit is moot.
  - `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
  - Future escape hatch (`add_with_factory(closure)`) is easy to add if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
- Generic `ActorZC<S>` / `ContextZC<S>` over the store backend.
  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
  - Type-erasure in `ContextZC::send` would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the measurement.
  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites unannotated.
- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
  - The runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later, predictably and once per dispatch.
- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
  - Function-pointer dispatch (`ActorLoopFn`) monomorphizes cleanly per `R`.
- Caller-supplied `Pool<S>`.
  - Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.

### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

## Future work: linkme/inventory benchmark harness

Idea captured during `0.3.0-0`; not scheduled for
implementation yet. Explores using compile-time static
registration (`linkme` distributed slices or `inventory` via
`ctor`) to build a `tprobe`-driven benchmark runner where
individual benchmarks declare themselves in separate files
and the runner picks them up with no `main()` edits per
benchmark — the ergonomic pattern criterion-rs offers.

### Fit for benchmarks

The bot thinks this is a strong fit. Each benchmark file
drops in a `bench!(...)` invocation; the runner iterates
the registered entries, sets up warmup/calibration/pin
config once, and prints a `tprobe` band table per
benchmark. The measurement primitive already exists
(`tprobe`); the missing piece is the registration and
iteration layer. Result: adding a benchmark is a
one-file change with no central table to edit.

### Fit for actor applications

The bot thinks this is a partial fit. Registration gives
discovery ("enumerate every `Actor` type the binary
knows"), but actor construction still needs runtime config
— id, name, channel endpoints, thread placement — which

1810	  - Criterion `iter_custom` over `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`.
1811	  - Reuses the `measurement * iters / total_count` scaling trick from `goal2-crit`.
1812	  - `Throughput::Elements(1)`.
1813	
1814	### Multi-step ladder
1815	
1816	- `0.5.0-0` — Plan marker.
1817	  - Bump `actor-x1` to `0.5.0-0`.
1818	  - This chores section.
1819	  - `notes/todo.md` entry under `## In Progress`.
1820	  - `CLAUDE.md`: add "Notes writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section (captures in-session feedback).
1821	  - No code change.
1822	- `0.5.0-1` — `pool.rs`: `Pool` + `PooledMsg` with `Drop` return path.
1823	  - Unit tests: round-trip, drop-returns, size enforcement.
1824	- `0.5.0-2` — `runtime_zc.rs`: `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` + `RuntimeZC` with both `run` and `run_no_probe`.
1825	  - Unit tests: ping-pong handling, clean shutdown, pool drain on exit.
1826	- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`.
1827	  - Install + smoke-run.
1828	- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
1829	  - `cargo bench` + spot-check throughput matches `goalzc`'s.
1830	- `0.5.0` — Closing marker.
1831	  - Drop `-N` suffix.
1832	  - Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md` Binaries + Benches.
1833	  - Move `notes/todo.md` entries to `## Done`.
1834	
1835	### Design decisions recorded here
1836	
1837	- Handler takes `&[u8]`; runtime owns the `PooledMsg`.
1838	  - Runtime receives `Signal::User(PooledMsg)` off the channel.
1839	  - Hands the handler `&*pooled` for the duration of `handle_message`.
1840	  - Drops the `PooledMsg` after the handler returns; buffer goes back to the pool.
1841	  - Pool traffic is one `get` + one `put` per dispatched message — exactly the cost goalzc exists to measure against goal2's unit-`Message` baseline.
1842	  - Passing `PooledMsg` by value into the handler would let a clever actor reuse the incoming buffer (avoiding pool churn).
1843	    - Rejected: `&[u8]` matches the shape of real zerocopy receivers and is the cleaner cross-validation target.
1844	- Fixed-size buffers (`MAX_SIZE`) for the MVP.

  - Install + smoke-run.
- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
  - `cargo bench` + spot-check throughput matches `goalzc`'s.
- `0.5.0` — Closing marker.
  - Drop `-N` suffix.
  - Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md` Binaries + Benches.
  - Move `notes/todo.md` entries to `## Done`.

### Design decisions recorded here

- Handler takes `&[u8]`; runtime owns the `PooledMsg`.
  - Runtime receives `Signal::User(PooledMsg)` off the channel.
  - Hands the handler `&*pooled` for the duration of `handle_message`.
  - Drops the `PooledMsg` after the handler returns; buffer goes back to the pool.
  - Pool traffic is one `get` + one `put` per dispatched message — exactly the cost goalzc exists to measure against goal2's unit-`Message` baseline.
  - Passing `PooledMsg` by value into the handler would let a clever actor reuse the incoming buffer (avoiding pool churn).
    - Rejected: `&[u8]` matches the shape of real zerocopy receivers and is the cleaner cross-validation target.
- Fixed-size buffers (`MAX_SIZE`) for the MVP.

For "the commit message" — I haven't actually drafted one yet this turn, so I want to make sure I'm looking at what you're looking at. Two candidates I can see:

If by "commit message" you mean the ladder entry at 1824, I'd update it to something like:

> `0.5.0-2` — `runtime_zc.rs`: `ActorManager` + `RuntimeZC` (catalog/transport split), `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits, `run` + `run_no_probe`.

For the chores section itself, the trim I'd propose:
- Keep the section title.
- Replace the multi-paragraph intro with a 2-sentence intro (what was added, the catalog/transport split is the headline).
- **Drop** the `### File-by-file` block entirely.
- **Keep** `### Tests added`.
- **Keep** `### Design decisions recorded here` — the *why* / *what was rejected* / *how it applies* shape is what makes this section worth re-reading later.
- **Keep** `### Future direction` — those are real notes for later.

Does that match what you have in mind, or are you pointing at a different "commit message"?

content.text:
Yes — splitting at 0.5.0 makes sense. Reasoning:

- **Semantic boundary**: 0.1.0–0.4.0 is "establish baseline measurements" (Stage 1 runtime, tprobe extraction, band-table polish, criterion cross-check). 0.5.0+ is the zerocopy/pooled/`ActorZC` track — genuinely a different architectural direction. Different file fits.
- **chores-01 is conceptually closed at 0.4.0**: the "Measurement precision" and "Future work: linkme/inventory" sections (lines 1653 and 2055) belong with the earlier-arc material.
- **Low cross-ref churn**: only `notes/todo.md` ref `[20]` points into the 0.5.0 ladder; trivial to retarget. No code references either chores file.
- **chores-01 stops growing**: future 0.5.0-3, 0.5.0-4, 0.5.0 (close), and any 0.6.x lands in chores-02 cleanly without bloating the older record.

For trim *within* chores-01 (the historical sections): I'd be cautious. Those capture decision rationale and are the source-of-truth for "why did we do X six versions ago". Aggressively rewriting them risks losing context that the bullet structure is already holding compactly. The biggest cheap win is the split itself plus the 0.5.0-2 file-by-file drop we already discussed (which moves to chores-02 in trimmed form anyway).

**Proposed plan**:

1. **Create `notes/chores-02.md`** — short header naming its scope, then move `## goalzc + RuntimeZC ... (0.5.0-0)`, `## Pool + PooledMsg (0.5.0-1)`, `## ActorManager + RuntimeZC ... (0.5.0-2)` from chores-01.
2. **Trim 0.5.0-2 during the move** — drop `### File-by-file`, tighten the intro, keep tests / design decisions / future direction.
3. **Refresh the stale `0.5.0-2` ladder line** inside the 0.5.0-0 plan marker section so it matches the shipped catalog/transport split.
4. **Update `notes/todo.md` ref [20]** from `chores-01.md#...` → `chores-02.md#...`.
5. Leave "Measurement precision" (line 1653) and "Future work: linkme/inventory" (line 2055) in chores-01 — both are about earlier-arc material.

This all lands as part of the 0.5.0-2 commit (it's the natural moment — the trim and the split serve the same review).

Sound good? If yes I'll make the changes and re-summarize for review.

1779	## goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong — plan marker (0.5.0-0)
1780	
1781	Adds a multi-threaded ping-pong workload whose messages are
1782	pool-backed byte buffers, rather than the unit `Message` that
1783	goal1 / goal2 exchange. Actors handle `&[u8]`; callers can
1784	cast inside their handlers via the `zerocopy` crate if they
1785	want typed views. A companion `goalzc-crit` criterion bench
1786	measures exactly the same work, probe-free.
1787	
1788	### Shape
1789	
1790	- `Pool` + `PooledMsg` — new `crates/actor-x1/src/pool.rs`.
1791	  - Buffers are fixed-size (`MAX_SIZE`) `Vec<u8>` owned by `PooledMsg`.
1792	  - `PooledMsg::Drop` returns the buffer to an `Arc<PoolInner>` shared across actor threads.
1793	  - Inner storage: `Mutex<Vec<Vec<u8>>>` — simple, some contention, upgradeable to lock-free later.
1794	  - `Deref<Target=[u8]>` so the handler's `&[u8]` is just `&*pooled`.
1795	  - Lazy allocation: pool starts empty; grows to steady-state (2 buffers for ping-pong) on demand.
1796	- `ActorZC` + `ContextZC` — new `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`.
1797	  - `trait ActorZC { fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC, msg: &[u8]); }`
1798	  - `trait ContextZC { fn get_msg(&mut self, size: usize) -> PooledMsg; fn send(&mut self, dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg); }`
1799	- `RuntimeZC` — same file.
1800	  - 1:1 thread-per-actor, mirroring `MultiThreadRuntime`.
1801	  - Channel payload is `Signal::User(PooledMsg)`.
1802	  - Two entry points:
1803	    - `run(warmup, measurement, pins) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented; for `goalzc`.
1804	    - `run_no_probe(warmup, measurement, pins) -> Vec<u64>` — probe-free; for `goalzc-crit`.
1805	- `goalzc` binary — new `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`.
1806	  - CLI mirrors `goal2`'s (`duration_s`, `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`).
1807	  - Adds `--size N` — message byte size; default 64; validated `1..=MAX_SIZE`.
1808	  - Prints throughput + tprobe band-table per actor.
1809	- `goalzc-crit` bench — new `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
1810	  - Criterion `iter_custom` over `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`.
1811	  - Reuses the `measurement * iters / total_count` scaling trick from `goal2-crit`.
1812	  - `Throughput::Elements(1)`.
1813	
1814	### Multi-step ladder
1815	
1816	- `0.5.0-0` — Plan marker.
1817	  - Bump `actor-x1` to `0.5.0-0`.
1818	  - This chores section.
1819	  - `notes/todo.md` entry under `## In Progress`.
1820	  - `CLAUDE.md`: add "Notes writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section (captures in-session feedback).
1821	  - No code change.
1822	- `0.5.0-1` — `pool.rs`: `Pool` + `PooledMsg` with `Drop` return path.
1823	  - Unit tests: round-trip, drop-returns, size enforcement.
1824	- `0.5.0-2` — `runtime_zc.rs`: `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` + `RuntimeZC` with both `run` and `run_no_probe`.
1825	  - Unit tests: ping-pong handling, clean shutdown, pool drain on exit.
1826	- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`.
1827	  - Install + smoke-run.
1828	- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
1829	  - `cargo bench` + spot-check throughput matches `goalzc`'s.
1830	- `0.5.0` — Closing marker.
1831	  - Drop `-N` suffix.
1832	  - Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md` Binaries + Benches.
1833	  - Move `notes/todo.md` entries to `## Done`.
1834	
1835	### Design decisions recorded here
1836	
1837	- Handler takes `&[u8]`; runtime owns the `PooledMsg`.
1838	  - Runtime receives `Signal::User(PooledMsg)` off the channel.
1839	  - Hands the handler `&*pooled` for the duration of `handle_message`.
1840	  - Drops the `PooledMsg` after the handler returns; buffer goes back to the pool.
1841	  - Pool traffic is one `get` + one `put` per dispatched message — exactly the cost goalzc exists to measure against goal2's unit-`Message` baseline.
1842	  - Passing `PooledMsg` by value into the handler would let a clever actor reuse the incoming buffer (avoiding pool churn).
1843	    - Rejected: `&[u8]` matches the shape of real zerocopy receivers and is the cleaner cross-validation target.
1844	- Fixed-size buffers (`MAX_SIZE`) for the MVP.
1845	  - Simpler pool (`Vec<Vec<u8>>`).
1846	  - Matches a ping-pong workload where every message is the same size.
1847	  - Size-classed sub-pools are future work; mentioned here explicitly so the extension isn't lost.
1848	- Lazy pool allocation.
1849	  - Pool starts empty; `get_msg` allocates a fresh `Vec<u8>` if the free list is empty.
1850	  - Steady state settles to 2 buffers for ping-pong after the first couple of dispatches.
1851	  - Pre-populating a fixed capacity is a micro-optimization that distorts first-dispatch latency (already dropped by warmup); deferred.
1852	- `--size N` CLI flag, not a compile-time const.
1853	  - goalzc is a benchmark; size sensitivity is exactly what a reader wants to sweep.
1854	  - `N` is validated against `MAX_SIZE` at startup.
1855	  - If `N > MAX_SIZE`, the binary errors cleanly rather than silently truncating.
1856	- `RuntimeZC` is a parallel runtime, not a replacement.
1857	  - `MultiThreadRuntime` stays untouched — existing `goal2` / `goal2-crit` keep working.
1858	  - `RuntimeZC` lives in its own module; no shared code with `MultiThreadRuntime`.
1859	  - Duplication is the price for a clean split that's easy to revisit once the shape stabilizes.
1860	  - The goal2-crit probe-contamination bug stays un-patched in `MultiThreadRuntime` for now; `run_no_probe` exists here from the start instead.
1861	- Multi-step `0.5.0` ladder.
1862	  - Five natural checkpoints: pool, runtime, bin, bench, close.
1863	  - Each reviewable on its own.
1864	  - A single-step `0.5.0` would be a large diff to review in one pass.
1865

1867	
1868	Implements the fixed-capacity buffer pool that `RuntimeZC`

## goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong — plan marker (0.5.0-0)

Adds a multi-threaded ping-pong workload whose messages are
pool-backed byte buffers, rather than the unit `Message` that
goal1 / goal2 exchange. Actors handle `&[u8]`; callers can
cast inside their handlers via the `zerocopy` crate if they
want typed views. A companion `goalzc-crit` criterion bench
measures exactly the same work, probe-free.

### Shape

- `Pool` + `PooledMsg` — new `crates/actor-x1/src/pool.rs`.
  - Buffers are fixed-size (`MAX_SIZE`) `Vec<u8>` owned by `PooledMsg`.
  - `PooledMsg::Drop` returns the buffer to an `Arc<PoolInner>` shared across actor threads.
  - Inner storage: `Mutex<Vec<Vec<u8>>>` — simple, some contention, upgradeable to lock-free later.
  - `Deref<Target=[u8]>` so the handler's `&[u8]` is just `&*pooled`.
  - Lazy allocation: pool starts empty; grows to steady-state (2 buffers for ping-pong) on demand.
- `ActorZC` + `ContextZC` — new `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`.
  - `trait ActorZC { fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC, msg: &[u8]); }`
  - `trait ContextZC { fn get_msg(&mut self, size: usize) -> PooledMsg; fn send(&mut self, dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg); }`
- `RuntimeZC` — same file.
  - 1:1 thread-per-actor, mirroring `MultiThreadRuntime`.
  - Channel payload is `Signal::User(PooledMsg)`.
  - Two entry points:
    - `run(warmup, measurement, pins) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented; for `goalzc`.
    - `run_no_probe(warmup, measurement, pins) -> Vec<u64>` — probe-free; for `goalzc-crit`.
- `goalzc` binary — new `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs`.
  - CLI mirrors `goal2`'s (`duration_s`, `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`).
  - Adds `--size N` — message byte size; default 64; validated `1..=MAX_SIZE`.
  - Prints throughput + tprobe band-table per actor.
- `goalzc-crit` bench — new `crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
  - Criterion `iter_custom` over `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`.
  - Reuses the `measurement * iters / total_count` scaling trick from `goal2-crit`.
  - `Throughput::Elements(1)`.

### Multi-step ladder

- `0.5.0-0` — Plan marker.
  - Bump `actor-x1` to `0.5.0-0`.
  - This chores section.
  - `notes/todo.md` entry under `## In Progress`.
  - `CLAUDE.md`: add "Notes writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section (captures in-session feedback).
  - No code change.
- `0.5.0-1` — `pool.rs`: `Pool` + `PooledMsg` with `Drop` return path.
  - Unit tests: round-trip, drop-returns, size enforcement.
- `0.5.0-2` — `runtime_zc.rs`: `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` + `RuntimeZC` with both `run` and `run_no_probe`.
  - Unit tests: ping-pong handling, clean shutdown, pool drain on exit.
- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`.
  - Install + smoke-run.
- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`.
  - `cargo bench` + spot-check throughput matches `goalzc`'s.
- `0.5.0` — Closing marker.
  - Drop `-N` suffix.
  - Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md` Binaries + Benches.
  - Move `notes/todo.md` entries to `## Done`.

### Design decisions recorded here

- Handler takes `&[u8]`; runtime owns the `PooledMsg`.
  - Runtime receives `Signal::User(PooledMsg)` off the channel.
  - Hands the handler `&*pooled` for the duration of `handle_message`.
  - Drops the `PooledMsg` after the handler returns; buffer goes back to the pool.
  - Pool traffic is one `get` + one `put` per dispatched message — exactly the cost goalzc exists to measure against goal2's unit-`Message` baseline.
  - Passing `PooledMsg` by value into the handler would let a clever actor reuse the incoming buffer (avoiding pool churn).
    - Rejected: `&[u8]` matches the shape of real zerocopy receivers and is the cleaner cross-validation target.
- Fixed-size buffers (`MAX_SIZE`) for the MVP.
  - Simpler pool (`Vec<Vec<u8>>`).
  - Matches a ping-pong workload where every message is the same size.
  - Size-classed sub-pools are future work; mentioned here explicitly so the extension isn't lost.
- Lazy pool allocation.
  - Pool starts empty; `get_msg` allocates a fresh `Vec<u8>` if the free list is empty.
  - Steady state settles to 2 buffers for ping-pong after the first couple of dispatches.
  - Pre-populating a fixed capacity is a micro-optimization that distorts first-dispatch latency (already dropped by warmup); deferred.
- `--size N` CLI flag, not a compile-time const.
  - goalzc is a benchmark; size sensitivity is exactly what a reader wants to sweep.
  - `N` is validated against `MAX_SIZE` at startup.
  - If `N > MAX_SIZE`, the binary errors cleanly rather than silently truncating.
- `RuntimeZC` is a parallel runtime, not a replacement.
  - `MultiThreadRuntime` stays untouched — existing `goal2` / `goal2-crit` keep working.
  - `RuntimeZC` lives in its own module; no shared code with `MultiThreadRuntime`.
  - Duplication is the price for a clean split that's easy to revisit once the shape stabilizes.
  - The goal2-crit probe-contamination bug stays un-patched in `MultiThreadRuntime` for now; `run_no_probe` exists here from the start instead.
- Multi-step `0.5.0` ladder.
  - Five natural checkpoints: pool, runtime, bin, bench, close.
  - Each reviewable on its own.
  - A single-step `0.5.0` would be a large diff to review in one pass.

## Pool + PooledMsg (0.5.0-1)

Implements the fixed-capacity buffer pool that `RuntimeZC`
