-- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod runtime_zc;` added after `pub mod runtime;`.
-  - `pub trait ActorZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.

-  - `pub trait ContextZC<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`. Send to closed channel silently drops the message (its `PooledMsg`'s buffer returns to the pool).
-  - `pub struct ActorManager<S: BufRefStore = MutexLifo>` — actor catalog.
-    - `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` — instance registration, no factory.
-    - `take_actors() -> Vec<Box<dyn ActorZC<S> + Send>>` — drained by Runtime at run time.
-    - `run(&mut self, mgr: &mut ActorManager<S>, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>` — tprobe-instrumented.

-    - `run_inner<R>` — shared orchestration body taking an `ActorLoopFn<S, R>` function pointer; `run` / `run_no_probe` differ only in which fn-ptr they pass. Function pointers are `Copy + Send + Sync`, so the orchestration stays generic without closure / `Fn`-bound complications.
-    - `MultiCtxZC<'a, S>` — borrows peer senders + pool; impls `ContextZC<S>`.
-### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)
-- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
-- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
-- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
-- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
-- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).
-  - The runtime's job is "move messages efficiently"; nothing else.
-  - The manager's job is "hold actor instances and assign ids"; nothing else.
-  - The two only touch each other through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called. A future swap of either side (M:N runtime, name-based discovery) leaves the other untouched.
-- Initial messages as a parameter to `run`, not state on either type.
-  - `run` neither stores them nor knows what they're for — it just delivers them after spawn / before warmup, then forgets.
-  - The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message"; that knowledge stays in the binary rather than leaking into runtime types.
-- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures.
-  - `ActorZC: Send` is already on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send on the trait" benefit is moot.
-  - `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
-  - Future escape hatch (`add_with_factory(closure)`) is easy to add if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
-  - Pool is generic over `S` since `0.5.0-1`; static dispatch all the way down preserves benchmark fidelity.
-  - Type-erasure in `ContextZC::send` would add a vtable hop per send, contaminating the measurement.
-  - Default `S = MutexLifo` keeps ergonomic call sites unannotated.
-- Handler takes `msg: &[u8]`; runtime owns the inbound `PooledMsg<S>`.
-  - The runtime's `actor_loop` calls `actor.handle_message(&mut ctx, &msg)` then `drop(msg)` — buffer returns to the pool one channel hop later, predictably and once per dispatch.
-- Two run methods rather than one with a `bool probe_enabled` parameter.
-  - A runtime branch on every dispatch would itself be a cost goalzc might want to measure or eliminate.
-  - Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
-  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
-  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
-  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
-  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
-- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
-  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
-- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
-  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
-  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
-  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.
-- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
-- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
-- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
-- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.
Idea captured during `0.3.0-0`; not scheduled for

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[20]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]

- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]

- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]

content.text:
**Naming**: underscore. Look at `runtime_zc.rs` / `band_table.rs` — Rust modules map `mod foo_bar` → `foo_bar.rs` natively, while `actor-manager.rs` would force a `#[path = "..."]` attribute just to avoid annoying Cargo. Hyphens in this repo are reserved for crate names (`actor-x1`, `tprobe`) and bench / bin *target names* declared in Cargo.toml (`goal1-crit.rs` is a bench target name, not a `mod`). So: `actor_manager.rs`.

**Splitting**: I lean yes, with a caveat about how to structure it. Reasoning:

- The headline of 0.5.0-2 was "split catalog from transport". The two types have disjoint responsibilities, no shared internal state, and connect only at `run`-time through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix`. File-level separation just finishes the split that types-level already made.
- You've already telegraphed that `ActorManager` will grow — "method-based API but also an Actor itself, likely multiple actors". One file gives that growth somewhere to land without the runtime's transport details polluting diffs.
- We're already touching this code in 0.5.0-2; doing the file split now is cheap and lands as part of the same commit. Doing it later is a separate refactor commit for marginal gain.

The caveat is **where the traits live**. `ActorZC` and `ContextZC` are consumed by both `ActorManager` (which holds `Box<dyn ActorZC<S>>`) and `RuntimeZC` (whose internal `MultiCtxZC` impls `ContextZC`). Three plausible layouts:

- **A** — traits stay in `runtime_zc.rs`; `actor_manager.rs` imports them. Cleanest mechanically but inverts the conceptual hierarchy: the manager owns the actor world, the runtime is "just a transport", but the traits would live in the transport file.
- **B** — move `ActorZC` + `ContextZC` to `actor_manager.rs` along with `ActorManager`; `runtime_zc.rs` imports them. Matches the conceptual hierarchy (actor model in one file; transport in another). My pick.
- **C** — traits in their own `actor_zc.rs`, manager in `actor_manager.rs`, runtime in `runtime_zc.rs`. Three files. Probably overkill for the volume we have today.

Tradeoff to flag: option B leaves `runtime_zc.rs` named after a trait (`ActorZC`) it no longer defines. Mild oddness; could rename later (`runtime_zc.rs` → `runtime_mt.rs` or similar) if you ever build a second transport.

Recommendation: **option B, file name `actor_manager.rs`, do it as part of 0.5.0-2** so the file-level split rides the same commit as the type-level split. If you'd rather defer (keep this commit code-stable, do the file move in 0.5.0-2.5 or fold into 0.5.0-3 prep), that's also defensible — just costlier in commit count.

toolUseResult.oldString:
## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime — `ActorZC` /
`ContextZC` traits, an `ActorManager<S>` catalog, a `RuntimeZC<S>`
transport, with `run` and `run_no_probe` entry points. The shipped
shape deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in three ways, all decided
during review:

- Two types instead of one — `ActorManager<S>` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC<S>` (transport). The runtime moves messages; the manager holds instances and assigns ids; they only touch through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called.
- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures. `ActorZC: Send` is on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send" benefit is moot; `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime. `run(&mut mgr, initial_messages, …)` takes them as a parameter; neither manager nor runtime stores them. The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message".

### Tests added (6 new in `runtime_zc::tests`, 28 total in lib)

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).
- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).
toolUseResult.newString:
## ActorManager + RuntimeZC: split catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)

Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime, split across two
modules with disjoint responsibilities:

- `actor_manager.rs` — actor model surface. `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits + `ActorManager<S>` catalog. Defines *what* an actor is and how the catalog addresses them.
- `runtime_zc.rs` — message-moving infrastructure. `RuntimeZC<S>` with `run` and `run_no_probe` entry points. One consumer of the actor model surface above; future transports (M:N scheduler, alternative channels) would be peers.

The shipped shape deviates from the `0.5.0-0` plan in four ways,
all decided during review:

- Two types instead of one — `ActorManager<S>` (catalog) + `RuntimeZC<S>` (transport). The runtime moves messages; the manager holds instances and assigns ids; they only touch through `take_actors` + `probe_name_prefix` when `run` is called.
- Two files instead of one — actor model surface lives in `actor_manager.rs`, transport in `runtime_zc.rs`. Reinforces the type-level split at the file level so future growth on either side (control-plane manager-actors; alternative transports) lands cleanly.
- Direct instance registration rather than factory closures. `ActorZC: Send` is on the trait, so the factory pattern's "don't require Send" benefit is moot; `mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 })` reads cleaner than `mgr.add_actor(|| PingPong { peer: 1 })`.
- Initial messages live in the app, not the runtime. `run(&mut mgr, initial_messages, …)` takes them as a parameter; neither manager nor runtime stores them. The benchmark / app is what knows "ping-pong needs one seed message".

### Tests added (6 new total, 28 lib tests)

In `actor_manager::tests`:

- `take_actors_drains_and_resets` — after `take_actors`, the manager is empty and follow-up `add` restarts ids from 0.
- `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix` — verifies the prefix accessor; full `"<prefix>.actor<id>"` composition is verified end-to-end via `goalzc`'s output later (TProbe's name is private).

In `runtime_zc::tests`:

- `ping_pong_runs_and_shuts_down` — two-actor ping-pong drives traffic for 40 ms each phase; both actors record nonzero counts.
- `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` — after `run` returns, every buffer that was ever in flight has come back; `pool.free_len() == pool.size()`.
- `run_no_probe_returns_counts` — probe-clean path runs the same workload, returns counts only, drains the pool the same way.
- `runtime_pool_accessor_returns_same_handle` — `RuntimeZC::pool()` exposes the caller's handle (verifies `msg_size` / `size`).

Updated summary:

**Code** (file split applied):
- `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml` — 0.5.0-1 → 0.5.0-2.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs` — added `pub mod actor_manager;` and `pub mod runtime_zc;`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs` (new, 151 lines) — `ActorZC` / `ContextZC` traits + `ActorManager<S>`. 2 tests (`take_actors_drains_and_resets`, `manager_exposes_probe_name_prefix`).
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs` (new) — `RuntimeZC<S>` transport + private internals (`SignalZC`, `MultiCtxZC`, `actor_loop_*`, `ActorLoopFn`). Imports the actor surface from `actor_manager`. 4 tests (ping-pong, drain, no-probe, pool accessor).

**Notes**:
- `notes/chores-02.md` (new) — moved 0.5.0-0/-1/-2 from chores-01. 0.5.0-2 section updated to reflect the two-file shape; ladder entry mentions both files; 4-deviation list (added "two files instead of one"); test list grouped by module.
- `notes/chores-01.md` — 2113 → 1837 lines, ends at 0.4.0 era + future-work notes.
- `notes/todo.md` — ref `[20]` retargeted to chores-02.

**Verified**:
- `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test` — 28 lib + 18 tprobe pass.
- `cargo install --path crates/actor-x1`; `goal1 --version` / `goal2 --version` both report `0.5.0-2`.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

11	
12	See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)
13	
14	- Initial design [[1]]
15	- PoC implementation of a manager
16	- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
17	  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
18	  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
19	- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
20	  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
21	  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
22	  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
23	  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
24	  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
25	  boost frequency first
26	- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
27	  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
28	  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
29	  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
30	  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
31	  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
32	  expected-scale heuristics
33	- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
34	  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
35	  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
36	  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
37	  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
38	- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
39	  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
40	  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
41	  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
42	  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
43	  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
44	  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
45	  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
46	  once we instrument drain cost):
47	  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
48	    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
49	    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
50	    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
51	    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
52	  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
53	    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
54	    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
55	    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
56	  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
57	    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
58	    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
59	    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
60	    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
61	  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
62	    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
63	    unbounded-growth shape.
64	- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
65	  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
66	      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
67	      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
68	      without the split;
69	  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
70	      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
71	      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
72	      same generated code under `--release`;
73	  (c) update the struct doc comment that references

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[20]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]

content.content:
- Lets the app build initial messages from the same pool the runtime uses.
  - Lets tests inspect `free_len` after shutdown to verify drain.
  - Lets `goalzc-crit` keep the pool across `iter_custom` calls (long-lived) while the runtime is rebuilt each call.
- `Send` bound on `ActorZC` (not `ContextZC`).
  - Actors get moved into spawned threads; must be `Send`.
  - `ContextZC` lives only inside one thread (constructed per-recv inside `actor_loop`); no `Send` needed.
- `let _ = sender.send(...)` in `MultiCtxZC::send` — closed channel drops the message silently.
  - The `Err(SendError(SignalZC::User(msg)))` case drops the `SignalZC`, which drops the inner `PooledMsg`, which returns the buffer to the pool — no leak.
- Drain assertion as part of the test contract.
  - `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` codifies "every PooledMsg that was ever in flight returns to the pool by the time `run` joins".
  - Non-obvious because it depends on `Drop for PooledMsg`, mpsc receivers dropping queued messages on close, and main-thread senders being dropped before join.
  - Codifying it now means a future change that breaks one of those steps gets caught immediately.

### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

toolUseResult.newString:
### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

## Hand-off — closing the 0.5.0 ladder (post 0.5.0-2)

Picks up where 0.5.0-2 leaves off; captures the as-built
API surface and the remaining steps. The 0.5.0-0 "Shape"
subsection above is the *original plan* — `0.5.0-1` and
`0.5.0-2` deviated from it (see those sections), so this
hand-off is the canonical starting point for the next
session rather than the plan-marker shape.

### Status

- `0.5.0-0` — plan marker. Done & committed.
- `0.5.0-1` — `Pool` + `PooledMsg` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo`. Done & committed.
- `0.5.0-2` — `actor_manager.rs` (catalog + traits) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport). Done; awaiting commit at hand-off time.
- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0` — closing marker. Pending.

### As-built API surface (deviations from `0.5.0-0` baked in)

- `Pool::new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32) -> Pool<MutexLifo>` — pre-allocates `msg_count` `Box<[u8]>` of `msg_size` bytes; no lazy growth, no `MAX_SIZE` const.
- `pool.get_msg(size: usize) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>` where `PoolError ∈ { SizeTooLarge { requested, max }, NoMsgs }`.
- `pool.msg_size() -> u32`, `pool.size() -> usize` — use `msg_size()` for `--size` validation; `NoMsgs` is the saturation signal at runtime.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs`:
  - `trait ActorZC<S = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `trait ContextZC<S = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`.
  - `ActorManager::new(prefix: &str)` / `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` / `take_actors() -> Vec<…>` / `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - `RuntimeZC::new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self` / `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
  - `run(&mut self, mgr, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores: &[usize]) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.
  - `run_no_probe(...) -> Vec<u64>` — same orchestration, probe-free hot loop.
- App pattern (drives both `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit`):
  ```rust
  let pool = Pool::new(size, 4);                    // 4 buffers ≫ ping-pong steady state of 2
  let mut rt  = RuntimeZC::new(pool.clone());
  let mut mgr = ActorManager::new("goalzc.dispatch");
  let a = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 });
  let _ = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 0 });
  let initial = vec![(a, pool.get_msg(size).expect("seed"))];
  let results = rt.run(&mut mgr, initial, warmup, measurement, &pins);
  ```

### 0.5.0-3 — `bin/goalzc.rs`

- CLI mirrors `goal2`'s positional / flags: `duration_s` (positional), `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`.
- New flag: `--size N` (`u32`, default 64). Validate `1 ≤ N ≤ pool.msg_size()` at startup; error cleanly if violated. The pool is constructed at runtime, so this is a runtime check, not a const.
- Pool sizing: 4 buffers covers ping-pong (steady state is 2). A `--pool-count` flag is a future tweak, not needed for 0.5.0.
- Use `RuntimeZC::run` (probe-instrumented).
- Output: throughput summary (use `tprobe::fmt::commafmt` or similar) + per-actor band tables. Crib pattern from `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`.
- Doc comments on `#[arg]` fields with bullet lists need `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, ...)]` per the CLAUDE.md clap caveat.
- Smoke-test post-install: `goalzc 1 --size 64`, `goalzc 1 --size 64 --pin 0,1`.

### 0.5.0-4 — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`

- Pattern: `iter_custom`, fresh runtime per sample, scaled by `measurement * iters / total_count`. Lift directly from `crates/actor-x1/benches/goal2-crit.rs`.
- **Use `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`, not `run`.** The lesson from `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination is the entire reason `run_no_probe` exists; calling `run` here would re-introduce the same bug.
- Add `[[bench]]` entry to `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`:
  ```
  [[bench]]
  name = "goalzc-crit"
  harness = false
  ```
- Default to `--size 64`. Sweeping sizes is optional — `Throughput::Bytes(size as u64)` if size-sweeping; `Throughput::Elements(1)` for fixed-size.
- Cross-check: run `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` at the same size; throughput should agree within the goal2-vs-goal2-crit window (~5–10%; `goal2`/`goal2-crit` reported 228 K vs 240 K msg/s during 0.4.0 development).

### 0.5.0 — close

- Bump `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml` from `0.5.0-N` → `0.5.0`.
- Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md`:
  - Binaries section: add `goalzc` (with `--size` description).
  - Benches section: add `goalzc-crit`.
- `notes/todo.md`: move the In-Progress `[20]` entry to `## Done` with reference `[20]`.
- Final `vc-x1 push main` to land the closing commit.

### Gotchas / non-obvious things

- **Drain invariant**: `pool.free_len() == pool.size()` must hold after every `run` / `run_no_probe` returns. Tests `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` and `run_no_probe_returns_counts` codify this; don't break them.
- **Probe contamination**: never call `RuntimeZC::run` from a criterion bench. Use `run_no_probe`.
- **`--size` validation** hits `pool.msg_size()`, not a const. The bound is set when the pool is built; the CLI pool gets built from the `--size` flag, so the validation is "is `--size` ≤ `pool.msg_size()` after pool construction" — easiest is `Pool::new(size, 4)` then nothing further to validate, since by construction `pool.msg_size() == size`. The validation matters more when sizes are runtime-variable (future multi-size sub-pools).
- **Bullet doc comments on `#[arg]` fields → reflowed to prose by clap unless `verbatim_doc_comment` is set.** See `vc-x1/src/init.rs` for worked examples; the CLAUDE.md "Writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section calls this out.
- **Initial messages are the app's responsibility** — neither manager nor runtime stores them. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` build the seed `PooledMsg` from the same `pool` they pass to `RuntimeZC::new`.
- **`PingPong` test struct**: lives in `runtime_zc::tests`. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` will need their own copy (it's small) or a shared `pub` version under `crate::actors` — leaving as a duplication choice for the next session.

### Future direction (noted, not built today)

- `ActorManager` itself becomes an `ActorZC` for control-plane work — a sibling actor on the same Runtime that handles "register a new actor at runtime", "lookup by name", "drain probes", etc.
- Multiple manager-actors for separate concerns (GUI, CLI, log drain, probe collect) all running on one Runtime.
- Discovery beyond `u32` ids — name-based, type-indexed.
- Closure-based init for richer post-spawn / pre-warmup work (`init: FnOnce(&Mailboxes<S>)`) if a workload ever needs more than a static `initial_messages` list.
- `add_with_factory` escape hatch if a `!Send`-during-construction actor type ever appears.

## Hand-off — closing the 0.5.0 ladder (post 0.5.0-2)

Picks up where 0.5.0-2 leaves off; captures the as-built
API surface and the remaining steps. The 0.5.0-0 "Shape"
subsection above is the *original plan* — `0.5.0-1` and
`0.5.0-2` deviated from it (see those sections), so this
hand-off is the canonical starting point for the next
session rather than the plan-marker shape.

### Status

- `0.5.0-0` — plan marker. Done & committed.
- `0.5.0-1` — `Pool` + `PooledMsg` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo`. Done & committed.
- `0.5.0-2` — `actor_manager.rs` (catalog + traits) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport). Done; awaiting commit at hand-off time.
- `0.5.0-3` — `bin/goalzc.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0-4` — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`. Pending.
- `0.5.0` — closing marker. Pending.

### As-built API surface (deviations from `0.5.0-0` baked in)

- `Pool::new(msg_size: u32, msg_count: u32) -> Pool<MutexLifo>` — pre-allocates `msg_count` `Box<[u8]>` of `msg_size` bytes; no lazy growth, no `MAX_SIZE` const.
- `pool.get_msg(size: usize) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>` where `PoolError ∈ { SizeTooLarge { requested, max }, NoMsgs }`.
- `pool.msg_size() -> u32`, `pool.size() -> usize` — use `msg_size()` for `--size` validation; `NoMsgs` is the saturation signal at runtime.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs`:
  - `trait ActorZC<S = MutexLifo>: Send` — `fn handle_message(&mut self, ctx: &mut dyn ContextZC<S>, msg: &[u8])`.
  - `trait ContextZC<S = MutexLifo>` — `get_msg(size) -> Result<PooledMsg<S>, PoolError>`, `send(dst_id: u32, msg: PooledMsg<S>)`.
  - `ActorManager::new(prefix: &str)` / `add<A: ActorZC<S> + 'static>(actor: A) -> u32` / `take_actors() -> Vec<…>` / `probe_name_prefix() -> &str`.
- `crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs`:
  - `RuntimeZC::new(pool: Pool<S>) -> Self` / `pool() -> &Pool<S>`.
  - `run(&mut self, mgr, initial_messages: Vec<(u32, PooledMsg<S>)>, warmup, measurement, pin_cores: &[usize]) -> Vec<(u64, TProbe)>`.
  - `run_no_probe(...) -> Vec<u64>` — same orchestration, probe-free hot loop.
- App pattern (drives both `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit`):
  ```rust
  let pool = Pool::new(size, 4);                    // 4 buffers ≫ ping-pong steady state of 2
  let mut rt  = RuntimeZC::new(pool.clone());
  let mut mgr = ActorManager::new("goalzc.dispatch");
  let a = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 1 });
  let _ = mgr.add(PingPong { peer: 0 });
  let initial = vec![(a, pool.get_msg(size).expect("seed"))];
  let results = rt.run(&mut mgr, initial, warmup, measurement, &pins);
  ```

### 0.5.0-3 — `bin/goalzc.rs`

- CLI mirrors `goal2`'s positional / flags: `duration_s` (positional), `--warmup`, `--ticks`, `--decimals`, `--pin`.
- New flag: `--size N` (`u32`, default 64). Validate `1 ≤ N ≤ pool.msg_size()` at startup; error cleanly if violated. The pool is constructed at runtime, so this is a runtime check, not a const.
- Pool sizing: 4 buffers covers ping-pong (steady state is 2). A `--pool-count` flag is a future tweak, not needed for 0.5.0.
- Use `RuntimeZC::run` (probe-instrumented).
- Output: throughput summary (use `tprobe::fmt::commafmt` or similar) + per-actor band tables. Crib pattern from `crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goal2.rs`.
- Doc comments on `#[arg]` fields with bullet lists need `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, ...)]` per the CLAUDE.md clap caveat.
- Smoke-test post-install: `goalzc 1 --size 64`, `goalzc 1 --size 64 --pin 0,1`.

### 0.5.0-4 — `benches/goalzc-crit.rs`

- Pattern: `iter_custom`, fresh runtime per sample, scaled by `measurement * iters / total_count`. Lift directly from `crates/actor-x1/benches/goal2-crit.rs`.
- **Use `RuntimeZC::run_no_probe`, not `run`.** The lesson from `goal2-crit`'s probe contamination is the entire reason `run_no_probe` exists; calling `run` here would re-introduce the same bug.
- Add `[[bench]]` entry to `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml`:
  ```
  [[bench]]
  name = "goalzc-crit"
  harness = false
  ```
- Default to `--size 64`. Sweeping sizes is optional — `Throughput::Bytes(size as u64)` if size-sweeping; `Throughput::Elements(1)` for fixed-size.
- Cross-check: run `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` at the same size; throughput should agree within the goal2-vs-goal2-crit window (~5–10%; `goal2`/`goal2-crit` reported 228 K vs 240 K msg/s during 0.4.0 development).

### 0.5.0 — close

- Bump `crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml` from `0.5.0-N` → `0.5.0`.
- Update `crates/actor-x1/README.md`:
  - Binaries section: add `goalzc` (with `--size` description).
  - Benches section: add `goalzc-crit`.
- `notes/todo.md`: move the In-Progress `[20]` entry to `## Done` with reference `[20]`.
- Final `vc-x1 push main` to land the closing commit.

### Gotchas / non-obvious things

- **Drain invariant**: `pool.free_len() == pool.size()` must hold after every `run` / `run_no_probe` returns. Tests `pool_is_full_after_shutdown` and `run_no_probe_returns_counts` codify this; don't break them.
- **Probe contamination**: never call `RuntimeZC::run` from a criterion bench. Use `run_no_probe`.
- **`--size` validation** hits `pool.msg_size()`, not a const. The bound is set when the pool is built; the CLI pool gets built from the `--size` flag, so the validation is "is `--size` ≤ `pool.msg_size()` after pool construction" — easiest is `Pool::new(size, 4)` then nothing further to validate, since by construction `pool.msg_size() == size`. The validation matters more when sizes are runtime-variable (future multi-size sub-pools).
- **Bullet doc comments on `#[arg]` fields → reflowed to prose by clap unless `verbatim_doc_comment` is set.** See `vc-x1/src/init.rs` for worked examples; the CLAUDE.md "Writing style: prefer sub-bullets" section calls this out.
- **Initial messages are the app's responsibility** — neither manager nor runtime stores them. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` build the seed `PooledMsg` from the same `pool` they pass to `RuntimeZC::new`.
- **`PingPong` test struct**: lives in `runtime_zc::tests`. `goalzc` / `goalzc-crit` will need their own copy (it's small) or a shared `pub` version under `crate::actors` — leaving as a duplication choice for the next session.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[20]]

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]

- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]

- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]

- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]

content.description: Add crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs: ActorZC + ContextZC traits, MultiThreadRuntimeZC with run() (tprobe-instrumented) and run_no_probe() entry points, channel carries Signal::User(PooledMsg). Mirror shape of MultiThreadRuntime. Unit tests for ping-pong handling + pool drain-on-shutdown.

content.subject: 0.5.0-3 goalzc binary
content.description: Add crates/actor-x1/src/bin/goalzc.rs. CLI mirrors goal2's (duration, --warmup, --ticks, --decimals, --pin) plus --size N for message byte size (default MAX_SIZE or 64). Prints throughput + tprobe band-table per actor.

content.subject: 0.5.0-4 goalzc-crit bench
content.description: Add crates/actor-x1/benches/goalzc-crit.rs. Criterion iter_custom over RuntimeZC::run_no_probe — probe-clean, measures exactly the same work goalzc runs. Same scaling pattern as goal2-crit.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

- goalzc + RuntimeZC: pooled zerocopy ping-pong (0.5.0) [[20]]
  - 0.5.0-0 plan marker — done
  - 0.5.0-1 `Pool` + `BufRefStore` + `MutexLifo` — done
  - 0.5.0-2 `actor_manager.rs` (catalog) + `runtime_zc.rs` (transport) — done; awaiting commit
  - 0.5.0-3 `bin/goalzc.rs` — pending
  - 0.5.0-4 `benches/goalzc-crit.rs` — pending
  - 0.5.0 close — pending
  - See [[21]] for as-built API surface, per-step starting points, and gotchas (canonical hand-off; supersedes the original `0.5.0-0` plan shape since `-1` and `-2` deviated from it)

## Todo

See [Foramt details](README.md#todo-format)

- Initial design [[1]]
- PoC implementation of a manager
- Explore `linkme` / `inventory` for a benchmark harness on top of
  `tprobe` — self-registering benchmarks (no `main()` edits per
  benchmark); possibly reusable for actor registration too [[9]]
- When a benchmark harness lands (criterion and/or the
  linkme/inventory idea at `[9]`), add a CPU-warmup helper to
  `tprobe` — probably `tprobe::warmup::cpu_boost(iterations)` or
  `cpu_boost(Duration)` — since `calibrate()` no longer warms
  the CPU itself. Harnesses that call `calibrate()` directly
  (without an app-level dispatch loop) need to prime the CPU to
  boost frequency first
- Add `--repeat N` flag to goal1/goal2 to run the full
  warmup+measurement sequence N times and report cross-run mean
  and stdev on `mean min-p99` / `adj mean min-p99`. Built-in
  replacement for the `for _ in 1..=30; do goal1 2 ...` shell
  loop; gives the empirical repeatability number A/B work
  depends on. See [[18]] for floors, technique, and
  expected-scale heuristics
- Revisit band-table `range` semantics — drop the `+1` so
  `first == last → range = 0` (true "no spread" instead of
  today's "1 tk / rounds to 0 ns"). `last - first` reads
  more naturally and matches what a reader expects "range"
  to mean. Pre-existing behavior, not introduced in 0.3.0-3
- **Bound `TProbe`'s records-buffer memory.** Each scope
  appends a 32-byte `Record` to an unbounded `Vec`; at
  `goal1 --inner 10` (~13M scopes/s), 20 s of measurement
  buffers 260M records ≈ 8 GB minimum, ~16 GB after
  `Vec` power-of-2 over-allocation, which swaps/OOMs on
  typical dev boxes. Pre-existing from 0.1.0-2 when the
  probe was vendored; becomes hard to miss at long
  measurement windows + small `inner`. Options (pick one
  once we instrument drain cost):
  - (a) **Hard cap + truncation counter.** Fixed max
    records (e.g. 64M ≈ 2 GB); past the cap, increment a
    dropped counter and skip the `push`. Report shows
    `truncated: N scopes dropped`. Preserves hot-path
    cost. Partial data on very long runs.
  - (b) **Periodic inline drain.** When records hit a
    threshold, drain into the histogram and continue.
    Memory bounded. Adds occasional drain stalls (ms-ish)
    that show up in the `p99-max` tail.
  - (c) **Inline drain, no buffer.** `end` / `end_batch`
    compute `per_event` and call `hist.record` directly.
    Fully bounded memory, no stalls, but adds ~15–20 ns
    to the hot path — non-trivial when the probe is
    measuring ~5 ns work per event.
  - (d) **Skinnier record (just `u64` per-event ticks).**
    4× memory reduction. Buys time but doesn't fix the
    unbounded-growth shape.
- Clean up `SingleThreadRuntime::run_for` inner loop:
  (a) remove the unnecessary `let Self { actors, queue, probe } = self;`
      destructure — disjoint `&mut` through named field paths
      (`self.actors[i]`, `self.queue`, `self.probe`) compiles
      without the split;
  (b) replace `while done < inner { …; done += 1; }` with
      `for _ in 0..inner { …; done += 1; }` (break on empty
      queue stays) — more idiomatic for a known upper bound,
      same generated code under `--release`;
  (c) update the struct doc comment that references
      "field-split-borrows actors and queue".

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Stage 1 runtime PoC [[2]]
- Extract perf into tprobe crate — plan marker (0.2.0-0) [[3]]
- Workspace layout + tprobe skeleton (0.2.0-1) [[4]]
- Move perf into tprobe crate (0.2.0-2) [[5]]
- Rename TProbe2 → TProbe (0.2.0-3) [[6]]
- Extract perf into tprobe workspace crate (0.2.0) [[7]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs — plan marker (0.3.0-0) [[8]]
- Notes reorg + per-crate READMEs + overhead-model.md (0.3.0-1) [[10]]
- Extend subtraction to framing + loop_per_iter (0.3.0-2) [[11]]
- Band-table raw columns + `adj mean` column (0.3.0-3) [[12]]
- Remove `--raw` flag, always calibrate (0.3.0-4) [[13]]
- Lower `--warmup` default 10 s → 0.5 s (0.3.0-5) [[14]]
- Band-table format + overhead docs (0.3.0) [[15]]
- Band-table decimals: default + `-d` override (0.3.1) [[16]]
- Comma-format summary counts (0.3.2) [[17]]
- Criterion benchmarks for goal1/goal2 (0.4.0) [[19]]

content.text:
Hand-off info added. Summary of this round:

**`notes/chores-02.md`** — appended `## Hand-off — closing the 0.5.0 ladder (post 0.5.0-2)` (~80 lines):
- **Status** — what's done, what's pending.
- **As-built API surface** — the canonical reference (since the `0.5.0-0` "Shape" subsection drifted as `-1` / `-2` deviated). Concrete signatures with file refs.
- **App pattern** — code snippet showing the `Pool` / `RuntimeZC` / `ActorManager` wiring, since both `goalzc` and `goalzc-crit` will use it.
- **0.5.0-3** — concrete CLI shape, validation, output pattern, install + smoke check.
- **0.5.0-4** — `iter_custom` template (lift from `goal2-crit`), reminder to use `run_no_probe`, Cargo.toml `[[bench]]` snippet, cross-check expectation.
- **0.5.0 close** — version bump, README updates, todo move.
- **Gotchas** — drain invariant, probe contamination, `verbatim_doc_comment` for clap bullets, initial-message ownership, `PingPong` duplication choice.

**`notes/todo.md`**:
- Expanded the In-Progress entry with sub-bullets for each remaining step.
- Added ref `[21]` pointing at the hand-off section.

Nothing about the code changed in this round — fmt / clippy / test from earlier still apply.

Two things to flag before you log off:

1. The 0.5.0-2 work (the catalog/transport file split + chores-02 creation + chores-01 trim) is **uncommitted on disk**. If you want it preserved across the night, that's a `vc-x1 push main` away — but it's also fine sitting in `jj`'s working copy and will be there in the morning.
2. If you'd rather pick up fresh tomorrow without first remembering "where was I", the cleanest thing is to commit before bed. Otherwise tomorrow's first task becomes "review tonight's diff again, then commit" — which is also fine, just a bit of context-reload overhead.

Your call. Sleep well.

content.text:
Sounds good. Proposed commit message for your approval:

**Title** (50 chars):
```
feat: split actor catalog from transport (0.5.0-2)
```

**Body**:
```
Adds the multi-threaded zerocopy actor runtime, split across
two modules — actor_manager.rs holds the actor model surface
(ActorZC / ContextZC traits + ActorManager catalog),
runtime_zc.rs holds the message-moving infrastructure
(RuntimeZC with run / run_no_probe). Also reorgs the 0.5.0
ladder into its own chores file.

- crates/actor-x1/Cargo.toml: 0.5.0-1 → 0.5.0-2.
- crates/actor-x1/src/lib.rs: + pub mod actor_manager; pub mod runtime_zc;.
- crates/actor-x1/src/actor_manager.rs: new — ActorZC / ContextZC traits + ActorManager catalog (2 tests).
- crates/actor-x1/src/runtime_zc.rs: new — RuntimeZC transport, SignalZC / MultiCtxZC / actor_loop_* internals, run + run_no_probe (4 tests).
- notes/chores-01.md: trim — moved 0.5.0-0/-1/-2 sections out (2113 → 1837 lines).
- notes/chores-02.md: new — 0.5.0 ladder relocated here; 0.5.0-2 section trimmed (file-by-file dropped, intro folded with deviations); hand-off notes for closing 0.5.0-3 / -4 / 0.5.0.
- notes/todo.md: ref [20] retargeted to chores-02; new ref [21] points at hand-off; In-Progress entry expanded with sub-ladder.
```

OK to push?

message.content: y
